title: genista cinerea subsp. cinerea
intro: Genista cinerea subsp. cinerea, also known as the Ashy broom or Ashy gorse, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub native to southern Europe and western Asia, with a history of use in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Genista cinerea subsp. cinerea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to create natural remedies.
